issues.body: The PLAN step calls `enumerate_provenance_orphans(repo, pulls, provenance)` without `bot_login`, so its default accepts every author ending in `[bot]`. The workflow later derives a concrete `BOT_LOGIN` from the successfully minted App token for CLAIM, and the new helper explicitly supports and tests this pin, but that identity is never provided during PLAN. A different GitHub App with write access can therefore open a same-repo `sparq-agent/...` PR and be counted as an inadmissible fleet provenance orphan, inflating `planned`, emitting warnings, and potentially driving zero-dispatch health alerts even though the PR was not created by this worker fleet.
issues.fix_hint: Expose the minted App slug/login to the PLAN step and pass it as `bot_login` to `enumerate_provenance_orphans`; fail closed if the worker bot identity cannot be established. Add an integration-level assertion covering the actual PLAN invocation so a different `[bot]` author is excluded in production wiring, not only when the helper is called manually with `bot_login`.
issues.title: Production orphan enumeration is not pinned to the worker App bot

summary: The orphan accounting is structurally validated and its tests are non-vacuous, but the production PLAN call does not supply the known App bot identity. Consequently the new alerting path classifies any same-repository, worker-ref-shaped PR authored by any GitHub App as this fleet's provenance failure. That makes the outstanding-work count and rolling health signal unsound and permits unrelated automation with repository write access to create persistent false provenance-orphan alerts. The helper and its self-test already support the required identity pin, so the production wiring must use it.
